summary: I ran the repo's standard checks against the pause-feature implementation. `bin/rubocop` passed clean (151 files, no offenses). `bin/rails test` (run with `PARALLEL_WORKERS=1` after the default fork-based parallel runner hung with zombie children in this sandbox — an environment quirk, not an app bug) completed 234 runs / 766 assertions with 3 errors, all in `test/services/phases/restart_define_test.rb`: three tests create a `step_runs` row with `iteration: 1` (and default `attempt: 1`) for the `requirements_writer` step, colliding with the `step_runs(:requirements_ready)` fixture that already occupies that same `(step_id, iteration, attempt)` slot, raising `ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique` on `index_step_runs_unique_without_shard`. I wrote `.pipeliner/phases/03-build/main/test-critic/verdict.json` with verdict `needs_work` and one blocker finding (F1) describing the fixture collision and exact repro command. No database outside the isolated `pipeliner_test`/`pipeliner_test_N` shards was touched at any point.
